ownership_map.effective_ownership_note: Top-10k ownership higher than overall (~75-80%). Consensus GW38 captain (35% armband poll, top captaincy rank factor, 6.9 xPts, 68% anytime-returns). Captaincy layer roughly doubles EO -> EO est ~120%+. Not owning AND not captaining = catastrophic green-arrow exposure.
Top-10k ownership ~60%. Secondary captain (CRF 13, ~50% returns proj). Modest captaincy share adds ~10-15pts EO -> EO est ~70%. Strong template hold; viable VC behind Haaland.

Status 'd' (75% chance), net -140k transfers out signals herd exit. Top-10k likely already trimmed below overall (~25-30%). EO est ~28%. Risk is asymmetric: if he plays and keeps a CS the held managers gain; if benched/absent the field has largely de-risked, so template damage to non-owners is shrinking.
EO est ~14%. Pens (order 1), set-piece taker, home FDR 2, xGI/90 0.56 -> best ceiling among sub-15% options. Mild differential rather than pure punt; meaningful rank lever if he hauls.
EO est ~7%. Secondary pens, FWD ceiling (14.0 xG), away FDR 3 tempers it. True sub-8% differential with goal upside.

EO est ~4%. Lowest-owned, home FDR 2, balanced xG/xA (13.6 xGI). Highest rank-up leverage if he returns; faces a Haaland-captained Villa, so a Rogers haul on a losing side is the contrarian dream scenario.

summary: GW38 template spine is Haaland + Salah; Haaland is the non-negotiable must-own AND must-captain (EO ~120%+ once armband share is added) -- not owning OR not captaining is the single biggest rank risk in the field. Salah is the safe template hold and best VC. Gabriel is a fading template asset (status 'd', heavy transfers out); ownership/EO is collapsing so the cost of NOT owning him is shrinking -- acceptable to be without. Best differentials are Rogers (4.1%, EO ~4%, home FDR 2, max leverage) and Mateta (6.8%, sub-8%, FWD ceiling); Mbeumo (14.2%) is a higher-floor near-differential with pens and set pieces. Recommended balance: lock the Haaland+Salah template core, captain Haaland, then deploy one true sub-8% differential (Rogers or Mateta) for rank-up upside while the field is over-invested in the doubtful Gabriel.
